perturbation.original_text: 16.1 This Agreement shall become effective on the date of execution and shall continue in force and effect for an indefinite term thereafter unless terminated pursuant to the provisions of Article 16 or by all the Parties in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: 16.1 This Agreement shall become effective on the date of execution and shall continue in force and effect for a term of five (5) years thereafter unless terminated pursuant to the provisions of Article 16 or by all the Parties in writing.
perturbation.explanation: Article 16.1 originally stated the agreement would continue for an indefinite term but it has been changed to continue for five (5) years, unless terminated. This introduces a contradiction, since some clauses are clearly intended to endure beyond five years, implying a longer-term relationship. Now, section 16.1 indicates the agreement lasts five years, while Article 14.2 indicates the duration of five (5) years after the termination of the agreement which creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14.2 MINDA/MIL, since it will have access to the Technical Know-How which it would not have had otherwise, expressly agree that: (a) during the term of the Agreement, and thereafter for a period of five (5) years after the termination of the Agreement (such termination being termination by IMPCO due to default by MINDA/MIL), MIL/MINDA shall not, directly or indirectly, either alone or collectively or through any of its associates, affiliates, including subsidiaries or any entity owned or controlled by it enter into another joint venture agreement or marketing/distribution agreement with any company or persons in respect of the marketing and sale of goods similar to the Products in the Territory. by using the Technical Know-How (b) the damages that would be suffered by IMPCO as a result of breach of the provisions of this Article may not be calculable and that an award of a monetary judgment for such a breach would be an inadequate remedy. Consequently, IMPCO shall have the right, in addition to any other rights it may have, to obtain, in any court of competent jurisdiction, injunctive relief to restrain any breach or threatened breach of any provision of this Article or otherwise to specifically enforce any of the provisions hereof. This remedy is in addition to any remedy for, and not in lieu of damages, directly or indirectly suffered by IMPCO and reasonable attorney's fees. (c) the duration for which the covenants in this Article are to be effective is reasonable. In the event that any court finally determines that the time period or the geographic scope of any such covenant is unreasonable or excessive and any covenant is to that extent made unenforceable, the parties agree that the restrictions of this Article shall remain in full force and effect for the greatest time period and within the Territory that would not render it unenforceable. The Parties agree that each of the covenants in Article shall be deemed to be a separate or independent covenant.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Article 16.1 specified an "indefinite term" for the agreement, while the changed text limits the agreement to a "term of five (5) years". Article 14.2 outlines obligations that extend for "five (5) years after the termination of the Agreement". If the agreement is only for five years as stated in the changed Article 16.1, the five-year period after termination described in Article 14.2 creates an inconsistency, as it implies a relationship that could extend beyond the initially defined term. This is a structural flaw causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 13.2 MINDA IMPCO Limited has agreed to allow the JVC, the right to use the two trade marks in respect of the products, the first being under the name of "IMPCO" and the second being in the name of "MINDA IMPCO". Unless otherwise agreed between the parties, the MINDA IMPCO trademark will be used on the products for sale within the Territory and the products exported would be sold with the trademark IMPCO.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.2 MINDA IMPCO Limited has agreed to allow the JVC, the right to use the two trade marks in respect of the products, the first being under the name of "IMPCO" and the second being in the name of "MINDA IMPCO". All products, regardless of sale location, will be sold with the trademark IMPCO.
perturbation.explanation: Article 13.2 originally stated the MINDA IMPCO trademark will be used for sale within the territory but now the trademark IMPCO will be used for the products, regardless of the sale location. This creates a contradiction, since this section stated the MINDA IMPCO trademark would be used for sale within the territory but now the trademark IMPCO will be used for all sales which creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.2 MINDA IMPCO Limited has agreed to allow the JVC, the right to use the two trade marks in respect of the products, the first being under the name of "IMPCO" and the second being in the name of "MINDA IMPCO". All products, regardless of sale location, will be sold with the trademark IMPCO.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Article 13.2 to state that all products, regardless of sale location, will be sold with the trademark IMPCO. This directly contradicts the original statement within the same Article 13.2, which specified that the MINDA IMPCO trademark would be used for sales within the territory. This creates a clear in-text contradiction because the changed text overrides and conflicts with the previously agreed-upon trademark usage for sales within the territory.

perturbation.original_text: 2.6 Subject to the approval of the Government of India (`GOI'), Reserve Bank of India (`RBI') and other Government agencies, if any, the shares in the JVC shall be issued to the Parties in the following percentage of the total paid-up capital of the JVC. MIL, MINDA and its associates - 40% - In cash and /or in Kind. IMPCO - 60% - In cash
perturbation.changed_text: 2.6 Subject to the approval of the Government of India (`GOI'), Reserve Bank of India (`RBI') and other Government agencies, if any, the shares in the JVC shall be issued to the Parties in the following percentage of the total paid-up capital of the JVC. MIL, MINDA and its associates - 40% - In kind. IMPCO - 60% - In cash
perturbation.explanation: Article 2.6 previously stated MIL, MINDA, and its associates would have their shares in the JVC issued by cash and/or kind, but it has been changed to only kind. This creates an in-text contradiction with the original intent of issuing shares in the JVC by cash and/or kind, since it now states that these shares shall be issued in kind. By making it just in kind, MIL, MINDA, and its associates are being limited on contributing to shares by cash which affects the original intent stated in the original agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.6 Subject to the approval of the Government of India (`GOI'), Reserve Bank of India (`RBI') and other Government agencies, if any, the shares in the JVC shall be issued to the Parties in the following percentage of the total paid-up capital of the JVC. MIL, MINDA and its associates - 40% - In kind. IMPCO - 60% - In cash
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the permitted form of share issuance for MIL, MINDA, and associates from "cash and/or in kind" to "in kind" only. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text (which is identical to the changed text), creating an in-text contradiction. The original intent of allowing share issuance through both cash and in-kind contributions is contradicted by the restriction to in-kind contributions only. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction because the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
